raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need bash command: find /testbed -name '*.java' -type f -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | tail -n1? Actually wc -l on multiple files prints total. So: find /testbed -name '*.java' -type f -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | tail -n1. But wc -l prints each file and total. We just want total. So: find ... | xargs -I{} wc -l {} | awk 'END{print $1}'. Simpler: find ... -type f -print0 | xargs -0 cat | wc -l. That counts lines across all. So command: find /testbed -name '*.java' -type f -print0 | xargs -0 cat | wc -l.
